behavior: Frequently walks or hops on open ground or in low vegetation, with near-constant tail-wagging; also gleans insects from foliage.
field_marks: Small, relatively upright and full-bellied warbler with long legs and tail
Dull brownish-olive upperparts and a slender, pointed dark bill
Bold pale eyebrow with a darker eye line
Yellow throat and yellow undertail coverts; belly varies from yellow to whitish
Rusty chestnut cap in breeding plumage; duller brown crown outside breeding season
Outer tail corners flash white in flight
habitat: Bogs and other open, wet woodland with scattered conifers and dense ground cover; outside breeding season also uses shrubby edges, weedy fields, hedgerows, and other open ground with low vegetation.

sources.title: Palm Warbler Identification — All About Birds
